titleStmt.title: The American Representative on the Advisory Council for Italy ( (Kirk) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The American Representative on the Advisory Council for Italy ( Kirk ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 49. From Reber. Reference Department’s 345, July 11, to Naples.Not printed; it requested a reply to instruction 897, March 25, 1944 (see footnote 30, p. 1482). A reply to Department’s telegraphic instruction No. 897, March 25, 1944, received through Political Advisor’s office AFHQ was made by memorandum to Algiers.

Assurances have been given by Italian Foreign Office that no records containing information of value to Allied Governments will be removed except by authorized representatives.See telegram 236, June 22, to Naples, p. 1486. Little information has been discovered as most of the confidential records were destroyed in September or removed to Republican Foreign Office in the north.The capital of the neo-fascist “Repubblica Sociale Italiana” was located at Said on Lake Garcia and the diplomatic corps was located at Brescia and at Venice Italian Foreign Office states that authorized representatives of Allied Governments are, of course, free to inspect existing files. A cursory inspection has been made by intelligence agencies in search for specific information. Little was found of any value. Two reports relating to
the German defense system have been removed and forwarded to G–2 AFHQIntelligence Section, Allied Force Headquarters. and 15 of telegrams relating to German withdrawal from the League of NationsOctober 14, 1933. was deposited with Political Section ACC.Allied Control Commission. Some additional information is being secured through search of records at former Ministry of Popular Culture. Full reports on these are being transmitted by mail to the Department. [Reber.]
